Joinder To Owen-Illinois, Inc.'S Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute
Off Calendar Nov-24-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT TENNECO AUTOMOTIVE OPERATING COMPANY, INC. Joinder to Owen-Illinois, Inc.'s Motion to Dismiss For Failure to Prosecute. Appearances: Christopher Hersom, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLC, (415) 898-1555, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Thomas A. Steig, Esq. of Prindle, Amaro, Goetz, Hillyard, Barnes & Reinholtz LLP (415) 788-8354 appearing for defendant and joinder party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: The motion of Owens-Illinois and all joinders thereto for discretionary dismissal of the wrongful death and children's loss of consortium claims are denied in the exercise of the court's discretion based on the lack of prejudice to defendants and plaintiffs' recent retention of counsel who is able to proceed with those claims. The motion of Owens-Illinois and all joinders thereto for mandatory dismissal of Jerold Arts' loss of consortium claim is granted since more than five years have expired and there is no exception to the mandatory five year rule. The motion of Owens-Illinois and all joinders thereto for mandatory dismissal of the survival claim is denied since the five year rule is tolled for the time from decedent's death to the filing of that claim. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/HEK) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR # 5797
